data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing — pin SHA, record verified_at_sha, in-toto-shaped HMAC); re-audit against the WHOLE roster incl. S2b janitor + S4b coherence]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S2b b73c, S3 fa49, S4b e308, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the attested-signing slice; the union of the EIGHT children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role):
- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 AC1/AC2 faithful read-tree+checkout-index materialize) + S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3/AC4 gate re-rooting onto the snapshot dir). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (it CONSUMES the pinned SHA; its Scope: "Out: ... snapshot/cache (S1/S2)"; "gate parameter threading (S3)"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC2 (a signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the SIGNED MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures still verify — and is reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered by THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER: its AC1 (verified-at-sha:<sha> MANIFEST STEP covered by the signature; NO _canonical_payload change, NO PAYLOAD_VERSION bump; regression test pre-existing signatures still verify; decision in ADR) + its AC2 (queryable verified_at_sha field; re-run against the same SHA reproduces a verdict+manifest over identical content; head_sha additive). Atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered SPLIT: THIS child owns the "CANNOT sign" half (its AC4: a local/dirty run never emits a signature, verify+report only; reinforced by the in-toto/local-bar wording). S3 (fa49 AC4) owns the "reads in-place checkout" half; the "documented back-out" clause is S3 (fa49 advisory AC "source=local documented as the rollback to in-place reads") + S6 docs. Clean reader/enforcer/doc split; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic/no-worktree population, no index.lock/config.lock contention) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close, watermark) + S2b (b73c janitor reclamation under disk pressure). This child's own AC3 (atomic+idempotent verified_at_sha pin via the ticket's compare-and-set event-append, overwrite-on-reverify, two-writer concurrency/retry test) is a finer-grained PIN-consistency guarantee that REINFORCES, not substitutes for, parent AC4's git/cache concurrency. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child for the git/cache layer. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 AC5 raises; attested fails closed) + S6 (1ba5 documents). This child reflects only the attested fail-closed-on-unresolvable-ref edge (its AC5: an unresolvable ref in attested mode fails closed, no signature) — adjacent to but not the credential-error obligation, which S1/S6 own. STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS+Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 AC4 impl) + S6 (1ba5 docs REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock caveat). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered by S2/S2b (watermark + grace/age windows) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via the standard rebar.toml + REBAR_* config precedence) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — PARTIALLY covered by THIS child + co-authored with S2: this child's AC1 records the MANIFEST-vs-PAYLOAD signing decision in the ADR (covering the "payload-schema SHA binding" rejected alternative — precisely this child's contribution); the PID+heartbeat-lease rejection + content-addressed layout + flock GC interlock + read-tree/checkout-index-over-git-archive are authored in S2 per the parent ADR note ("authored in S2/S4"). S4b (e308) carries ADR 0002 (claim-gate<->plan-review coherence). This child cleanly covers its named slice. Not an absence (S2/S4b cover the rest).
- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this child is S4 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3/AC8. The "signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage)" clause is PRECISELY this child's core deliverable (its AC1 manifest-step binding + no-PAYLOAD_VERSION-bump + prior-sig regression test; AC2 reproducible re-run; AC6 in-toto Statement envelope so HMAC->DSSE is an envelope swap). The "local preserves verify-before-push" clause is reinforced by this child's signing bar (its AC4). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer hand-off (covered-by-named-consumer, not an absence): this child's HMAC-trust-model + in-toto-shape DOCS are explicitly delegated to S6 ("limits documented in S6"); S6 AC4 claims that obligation. The claim-gate<->plan-review ref-resolution coherence (the larger coherence story, not the attested-signing pin) is explicitly delegated to the SIBLING S4b (e308) by this child's own Why/Scope ("Claim-gate ref-resolution coherence is a sibling story; this slice is the attested-signing pin only" / "Out: claim-gate ref-resolution coherence (sibling story)"); S4b is present in the roster and owns it. No obligation named here is left unclaimed.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on this child — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check across the S3<->S4(/S4b) seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, and the ref-coherence clause delegated to S4b) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the attested-signing slice (manifest-channel SHA binding with no payload-version bump + prior-signature preservation, verified_at_sha record + lifecycle, atomic/idempotent/overwrite-on-reverify pin via compare-and-set, unresolvable-ref fail-closed, local-mode signing bar, in-toto Statement envelope, the payload-schema-rejection slice of the ADR) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC2 and the "cannot-sign" half of AC3, and contributes the manifest-vs-payload decision slice of AC8. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
